test.name: Historical event lookup
test.description: Wikipedia lookup for a genealogically relevant historical event (the Great Famine in Ireland). Exercises a topic whose title contains parentheses, testing slug normalization.

input.user_message: I'm researching an Irish ancestor who emigrated around 1850. Can you look up the Great Famine in Ireland on Wikipedia?

judge_context: The returned title is 'Great Famine (Ireland)' — parentheses collapse into hyphens, producing slug 'great-famine-ireland'. The extract must be used verbatim, not summarized.

IMPORTANT — file visibility: This skill writes a standalone markdown file to the working folder. That file will NOT appear in the file changes summary (which only tracks research.json and tree.gedcomx.json). If the skill called wikipedia_search successfully and its text response confirms saving a file with a correctly slugified name, treat file creation as fully successful. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for '(no file changes)' — that is expected behavior for this skill.

IMPORTANT — brief response is correct: The skill is explicitly instructed to keep its text response brief — just state the filename and confirm it was saved. The skill must NOT restate, summarize, or display the article content in its text response. A short confirmation like 'Saved the Wikipedia summary to great-famine-ireland.md' is the CORRECT and COMPLETE response. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for a brief response that omits article content.
